title: Model Router Extension — Audit & Fix Plan
lede: Comprehensive code audit of the model-router extension found 14 issues across 3 severity tiers. This plan addresses the 10 actionable fixes, prioritized by risk, with test coverage gaps identified for follow-up.

sections.heading: Findings Summary
sections.html:
#IssueSeverityEffortFile
1Budget check bypassed when tier pinnedCriticalSmallrouting.ts
2wordCount shadowing in decideRoutingHighTrivialrouting.ts
3resolveAvailableTier returns non-existent tierHighSmallrouting.ts
4getThinkingOverride returns anyMediumTrivialprovider.ts
5streamSimple cast to anyMediumSmallprovider.ts
6handleFix no tier validationMediumTrivialcommands.ts
7handleDisable router-model loopMediumTrivialcommands.ts
8Rough token estimationLowDocprovider.ts
9Classifier truncation at 300 charsMediumMediumrouting.ts
10persistState dedup raceLowTrivialindex.ts

sections.heading: Critical / High Fixes
sections.html:
These three fixes address the most impactful issues. Fix #1 is the only one that could cause real financial harm.

Fix #1: Budget check bypassed when tier is pinned

File: extensions/model-router/routing.ts — decideRouting

Problem: The budget check is inside the else block of the if (pinnedTier) conditional. When a user pins "high" via /router pin high, the budget check is entirely skipped, allowing unbounded costs.

Approach: Move the budget check outside the if/else so it always applies regardless of pin state. The pinned tier should be respected, but the budget override should still be enforced as a safety cap.

// Current (broken):
if (pinnedTier) {
  tier = pinnedTier;
} else {
  // ... heuristics ...
  if (isBudgetExceeded && tier === "high") {
    tier = "medium"; // budget check only in else
  }
}

// Fixed:
if (pinnedTier) {
  tier = pinnedTier;
} else {
  // ... heuristics ...
}
// Budget check always applies:
if (isBudgetExceeded && tier === "high") {
  tier = "medium";
  phase = "implementation";
  reasoning = `Budget exceeded. Downgraded from high to medium tier. (Original: ${reasoning})`;
  isBudgetForced = true;
}

Fix #2: Remove wordCount shadowing

File: extensions/model-router/routing.ts — decideRouting

Problem: const wordCount is declared at the top of the function, then redeclared inside the if (rules && wordCount < 100) block. The inner declaration shadows the outer one and is redundant.

Approach: Remove the inner const wordCount = countWords(prompt) — the outer one is already computed and used in the condition.

Fix #3: resolveAvailableTier returns non-existent tier

File: extensions/model-router/routing.ts — resolveAvailableTier

Problem: When no tier exists in the profile, the function returns the preferred tier anyway. This causes buildRoutingDecision to throw.

Approach: Return undefined when no tier is available, and handle the undefined case in decideRouting by falling back to a default tier with a warning.

sections.heading: Medium Fixes
sections.html:
Fix #4: getThinkingOverride return type

File: extensions/model-router/provider.ts

Approach: Change the return type from any to ThinkingLevel | undefined.

Fix #5: Remove as any from provider registration

File: extensions/model-router/provider.ts

Approach: Properly type the streamSimple method signature using the types from @earendil-works/pi-ai. This may require importing the correct types and ensuring the implementation matches the interface.

Fix #6: handleFix tier validation

File: extensions/model-router/commands.ts — handleFix

Approach: Before pinning, check that the profile actually has the specified tier configured. If not, show a warning and skip the pin.

Fix #7: handleDisable router-model guard

File: extensions/model-router/commands.ts — handleDisable

Approach: After parsing state.lastNonRouterModel, check that the provider is not "router". If it is, show a warning and suggest using /model directly.

Fix #9: Classifier prompt truncation

File: extensions/model-router/routing.ts — runClassifier

Approach: Instead of truncating the first 300 chars, keep the last 300 chars (where the actual task intent is more likely to be). Alternatively, use a smarter strategy: extract the last user message and use that as the primary context, with recent conversation as secondary context.

sections.heading: Low / Code Quality
sections.html:
Fix #8: Token estimation documentation

File: extensions/model-router/provider.ts — estimateTokens

Approach: Add a /** Rough estimate — not accurate for code-heavy prompts */ comment explaining that the 33% ratio is a heuristic.

Fix #10: persistState in-flight guard

File: extensions/model-router/index.ts — persistState

Approach: Add a boolean flag isPersisting that is set to true before pi.appendEntry and reset in a finally block. Skip the write if already in-flight.

Additional code quality items

  • Fix #11: Make handleDebug toggle explicit with cmd === "toggle"
  • Fix #12: Extract magic constants in decideRouting to named constants with doc comments
  • Fix #13: Pre-compile keyword regexes in containsAny for performance

sections.heading: Test Coverage Gaps
sections.html:
The existing 48 tests cover config.ts and routing.ts core logic well, but miss significant areas:

  • loadRouterConfig — full config loading pipeline (global + project merge, normalization)
  • normalizeTierConfig — model aliases, fallbacks, reasoning, thinking levels
  • normalizeModelsMap — edge cases (missing model field, invalid contextWindow, etc.)
  • resolveContextWindow / resolveMaxOutputTokens — registry lookup fallback
  • hasImageAttachment — content part detection
  • getRecentConversationText — message slicing and filtering
  • countToolResults — role-based filtering
  • Extension lifecycle (index.ts) — session start, model select, turn end
  • Provider registration (provider.ts) — stream handling, fallback chains
  • Command handlers (commands.ts) — all subcommand paths
  • Status widget (ui.ts) — rendering edge cases

Recommendation: Add integration-style tests for the provider and command layers using mocked ExtensionContext and ExtensionAPI.

sections.heading: Implementation Order
sections.html:
Recommended order to minimize risk and maximize impact:

  1. Fix #1 (budget bypass) — highest risk, smallest change
  2. Fix #2 (wordCount shadowing) — trivial, cleans up confusion
  3. Fix #3 (resolveAvailableTier) — prevents potential crash
  4. Fix #6 (handleFix validation) — trivial, improves UX
  5. Fix #7 (handleDisable guard) — trivial, prevents loop
  6. Fix #10 (persistState guard) — trivial, improves robustness
  7. Fix #4 (getThinkingOverride type) — type safety improvement
  8. Fix #5 (provider typing) — type safety improvement
  9. Fix #8 (token estimation docs) — documentation only
  10. Fix #9 (classifier truncation) — medium effort, design decision
  11. Fix #11-13 — code quality, can be done in batch
  12. Add missing tests — separate PR or batch after fixes land
